dtcs.description: Control Module Communication Bus OFF
Lost Communication with Clearance Warning ECU There DTCs are stored when a malfunction occurs in the CAN communication circuit. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK DTC (a) Clear the DTC    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Navigation System/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Recheck for DTCs and check if the same trouble occurs again. OK: No DTCs are output. NG --  GO TO CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/CAN Communication System/How to Proceed With OK --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting Vehicle Stability Control System

dtcs.description: 62, U0124/95, U0126/63, U0130/68: Control Module Communication Bus OFF

Lost Communication with Steering Effort Control Module If 2 or more DTCs are output during the DTC check, one side of the CAN branch wire may be open (One side of the CANH [branch wire]/CANL [branch wire] of the ECU and/or sensor is open). NOTICE: Inspect the fuses for circuits related to this system before performing the following inspection procedure. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: DISCONNECT CABLE FROM NEGATIVE BATTERY TERMINAL (a) Disconnect the cable from the negative (-) battery terminal before measuring the resistances of the main wire and the branch wire.

CHECK FOR OPEN IN CAN BUS WIRE (STEERING CONTROL ECU BRANCH WIRE) (a) Disconnect the E84 steering control ECU connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE STEERING CONTROL ECU BRANCH WIRE OR CONNECTOR (CANH, CANL) OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (STEERING CONTROL ECU - BATTERY AND BODY GROUND) (a) Connect the cable to the negative (-) battery terminal. NOTICE: When disconnecting the cable, some systems need to be initialized after the cable is reconnected    See: Testing and Inspection/Repair Instruction - Initialization. (b) Disconnect the E82 and E83 steering control ECU connectors. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: (d) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Voltage: Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK --  REPLACE STEERING CONTROL ECU  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Steering and Suspension/Relays and Modules - Steering/Steering Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal

dtcs.description: Lost Communication with ECM / PCM "A" The combination meter communicates with the ECM via the CAN line. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK FOR DTC (a) Check for DTCs and note any codes that are output    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Clear the DTCs    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (c) Recheck for DTCs    See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result HINT If CAN communication system DTC U0100 and another DTC are output at the same time, perform troubleshooting for CAN communication system DTC U0100 first. B --  Go to CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Service Precautions A --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting Pre-Collision System

dtcs.description: Lost Communication with Distance Control ECU The seat belt control ECU receives signals from the master cylinder solenoid (skid control ECU) and the center airbag sensor via the CAN communication line. The distance control ECU receives signals from the ECM via the CAN communication line. HINT When 2 or more DTCs starting with "U" are output simultaneously, inspect the connectors and wire harnesses of each ECU. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK DTC (CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM) (a) Use the Techstream to check if the CAN communication system is functioning normally    See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/CAN Communication System/How to Proceed With Troubleshooting. OK: DTC is not output. NG --  GO TO CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/CAN Communication System/Diagnosis System OK --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting Active Height Control Suspension

CHECK FOR OPEN IN CAN BUS WIRE (DISTANCE CONTROL ECU ASSEMBLY) (a) Disconnect the E86 distance control ECU assembly connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE DISTANCE CONTROL ECU ASSEMBLY BRANCH WIRE OR CONNECTOR (CANH, CANL) OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (DISTANCE CONTROL ECU ASSEMBLY - BATTERY AND BODY GROUND) (a) Connect the cable to the negative (-) battery terminal. NOTICE: When disconnecting the cable, some systems need to be initialized after the cable is reconnected    See: Testing and Inspection/Repair Instruction - Initialization. (b) Disconnect the E86 distance control ECU assembly connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: (d) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Voltage: Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK --  REPLACE DISTANCE CONTROL ECU ASSEMBLY  See: Cruise Control/Cruise Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal Dynamic Radar Cruise Control System

dtcs.description: Lost Communication with Main Body ECU The suspension control ECU receives signals from the ECM, four wheel drive control ECU, skid control ECU, yaw rate sensor, steering sensor, steering control ECU and main body ECU via CAN communication. When DTCs indicating a CAN communication system malfunction are output, repair the CAN communication system first. NOTICE: * Before performing troubleshooting, inspect the connectors of related circuits. * If the suspension control ECU or height control sensor is replaced, the vehicle height offset calibration must be performed See: Steering and Suspension/Suspension/Suspension Control ( Automatic - Electronic )/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CLEAR DTC (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Steering and Suspension/Suspension/Suspension Control ( Automatic - Electronic )/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC (a) Perform a road test. (b) Check for DTCs. OK: DTC is not output. NG --  GO TO CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM (HOW TO PROCEED WITH TROUBLESHOOTING)  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/CAN Communication System/How to Proceed With Troubleshooting OK --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting Meter / Gauge System

dtcs.description: Lost Communication with Cruise Control Front Distance Range Sensor The distance control ECU sends general vehicle control information, such as vehicle speed, steering angle, etc., to the millimeter wave radar sensor. The millimeter wave radar sensor sends information about whether another vehicle is present in front of the vehicle, the distance between the vehicles, relative speed, etc. to the distance control ECU. The distance control ECU sends this information to the ECM and performs control of cruise control. NOTICE: * When the distance control ECU is replaced with a new one, initialization must be performed See: Cruise Control/Cruise Control Module/Testing and Inspection. * When the millimeter wave radar sensor is replaced with a new one, adjustment of the radar sensor beam axis must be performed See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Distance Sensor/Adjustments. * As it is possible that this DTC was stored due to a bad connection of a connector, make sure to check that each connector is connected securely. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (MILLIMETER WAVE RADAR SENSOR - DISTANCE CONTROL ECU) (a) Disconnect the A44 millimeter wave radar sensor connector. (b) Disconnect the E86 distance control ECU connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DISTANCE CONTROL ECU (a) Disconnect the A44 millimeter wave radar sensor connector. (b) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Voltage: NG --  REPLACE DISTANCE CONTROL ECU  See: Cruise Control/Cruise Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal OK -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE MILLIMETER WAVE RADAR SENSOR (a) Replace the millimeter wave radar sensor with a new one    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Distance Sensor/Service and Repair/Removal. (b) Adjust the millimeter wave radar sensor    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Distance Sensor/Adjustments. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC (DYNAMIC RADAR CRUISE CONTROL SYSTEM) (a) Using the Techstream, enter the following menus: Powertrain / Radar Cruise / Trouble Code. (b) Clear the DTCs    See: Cruise Control/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Dynamic Radar Cruise Control System. (c) Perform the following to make sure the DTC detection conditions are met. HINT If the detection conditions are not met, the system cannot detect the malfunction. (1) Drive the vehicle at a speed of 40 km/h (25 mph) or more. (2) Turn the cruise control main switch on. (3) Push the -SET switch to activate the dynamic radar cruise control. (d) Check for DTCs    See: Cruise Control/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Dynamic Radar Cruise Control System. OK:

dtcs.description: Lost Communication with Radar Sensor The distance control ECU sends the vehicle speed and estimated curve information to the millimeter wave radar sensor. The millimeter wave radar sensor sends information about whether another vehicle is present in front of the vehicle, the distance between the vehicles, relative speed, etc. to the distance control ECU. The distance control ECU sends this information to the ECM and performs control of cruise control. NOTICE: * When the distance control ECU is replaced with a new one, initialization must be performed See: Cruise Control/Cruise Control Module/Testing and Inspection. * When the millimeter wave radar sensor is replaced with a new one, adjustment of the radar sensor beam axis must be performed See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Collision Avoidance and Parking Assist Systems/Distance Sensor/Adjustments. * As it is possible that this DTC was stored due to a bad connection of a connector, make sure to check that each connector is connected securely. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (DISTANCE CONTROL ECU - MILLIMETER WAVE RADAR SENSOR) (a) Disconnect the A44 millimeter wave radar sensor connector. (b) Disconnect the E86 distance control ECU connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE DISTANCE CONTROL ECU (a) Replace the distance control ECU with a new one    See: Cruise Control/Cruise Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal. (b) Perform initialization    See: Cruise Control/Cruise Control Module/Testing and Inspection. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC (DYNAMIC RADAR CRUISE CONTROL SYSTEM) (a) Using the Techstream, enter the following menus: Powertrain / Radar Cruise / Trouble Code. (b) Clear the DTCs    See: Cruise Control/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Dynamic Radar Cruise Control System. (c) Perform the following to make sure the DTC detection conditions are met. HINT If the detection conditions are not met, the system cannot detect the malfunction. (1) Drive the vehicle at a speed of 40 km/h (25 mph) or more. (2) Turn the cruise control main switch on. (3) Push the -SET switch to activate the dynamic radar cruise control. (d) Check for DTCs    See: Cruise Control/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Dynamic Radar Cruise Control System. OK:

dtcs.description: Software Incompatibility with Vehicle Security Control Module NOTICE: Inspect the fuses for circuits related to this system before performing the following inspection procedure. PROCEDURE If 2 or more DTCs are output during the DTC check, one side of the CAN branch wire may be open (one side of the CANH [branch wire]/CANL [branch wire] of the ECU and/or sensor is open). * *1: w/ Power Back Door System If 2 or more DTCs are output during the DTC check, one side of the CAN branch wire may be open (one side of the CANH [branch wire]/CANL [branch wire] of the ECU and/or sensor is open). * *1: w/ Dynamic Radar Cruise Control System * *2: w/ Wide View Front and Side Monitor System

steps.instruction: DISCONNECT CABLE FROM NEGATIVE BATTERY TERMINAL (a) Disconnect the cable from the negative (-) battery terminal before measuring the resistances of the main wire and the branch wire.

CHECK FOR OPEN IN CAN BUS WIRE (CERTIFICATION ECU BRANCH WIRE) (a) Disconnect the E29 certification ECU (smart key ECU) connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE CERTIFICATION ECU (SMART KEY ECU) BRANCH WIRE OR CONNECTOR (CANH, CANL) OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (CERTIFICATION ECU - BATTERY AND BODY GROUND) (a) Connect the cable to the negative (-) battery terminal. NOTICE: When disconnecting the cable, some systems need to be initialized after the cable is reconnected    See: Testing and Inspection/Repair Instruction - Initialization. (b) Disconnect the E29 certification ECU (smart key ECU) connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: (d) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Voltage: Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK --  REPLACE CERTIFICATION ECU ASSEMBLY (SMART KEY ECU)  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Alarm Module/Service and Repair/Removal Part 1 NETWORKING: CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM: U1002 (CAN MS BUS): Lost Communication with Gateway Module (Main body ECU) U1002 (CAN MS BUS) - Lost Communication with Gateway Module (Main body ECU) PROCEDURE

CHECK FOR OPEN IN CAN BUS MAIN WIRE (NO. 2 CAN JUNCTION CONNECTOR - COMBINATION METER) (a) Disconnect the L30 No. 2 CAN junction connector connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  CONNECT CONNECTOR OK --  REPAIR OR REPLACE NO. 2 CAN JUNCTION CONNECTOR
CONNECT CONNECTOR (a) Reconnect the L29 No. 2 CAN junction connector connector. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR OPEN IN CAN BUS MAIN WIRE (NO. 8 JUNCTION CONNECTOR - MAIN BODY ECU) (a) Disconnect the E76 No. 8 junction connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  CONNECT CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR OPEN IN CAN BUS MAIN WIRE (NO. 8 JUNCTION CONNECTOR - NO. 2 CAN JUNCTION CONNECTOR) (a) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE CAN MAIN WIRE OR CONNECTOR (NO. 8 JUNCTION CONNECTOR - NO. 2 CAN JUNCTION CONNECTOR) OK --  REPAIR OR REPLACE NO. 8 JUNCTION CONNECTOR
CONNECT CONNECTOR (a) Reconnect the E76 No. 8 junction connector connector. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR OPEN IN CAN BUS MAIN WIRE (MAIN BODY ECU - NO. 8 JUNCTION CONNECTOR) (a) Disconnect the E3 main body ECU (cowl side junction block LH) connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE CAN MAIN WIRE CONNECTED TO MAIN BODY ECU (COWL SIDE JUNCTION BLOCK LH) (MAIN BODY ECU - NO. 8 JUNCTION CONNECTOR) OK --  REPLACE MAIN BODY ECU (COWL SIDE JUNCTION BLOCK LH)
CONNECT CONNECTOR (a) Reconnect the L29 and L30 No. 2 CAN junction connector connectors. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR OPEN IN CAN BUS MAIN WIRE (NO. 9 JUNCTION CONNECTOR - NO. 10 JUNCTION CONNECTOR) (a) Disconnect the E70 No. 10 junction connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  CONNECT CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR OPEN IN CAN BUS MAIN WIRE (NO. 10 JUNCTION CONNECTOR - COMBINATION METER) (a) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  CONNECT CONNECTOR OK --  REPAIR OR REPLACE NO. 10 CAN JUNCTION CONNECTOR
CONNECT CONNECTOR (a) Reconnect the E70 No. 10 junction connector connector. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR OPEN IN CAN BUS MAIN WIRE (COMBINATION METER - NO. 10 JUNCTION CONNECTOR) (a) Disconnect the E77 combination meter connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE CAN MAIN WIRE CONNECTED TO COMBINATION METER (COMBINATION METER - NO. 10 JUNCTION BLOCK) OK --  REPLACE COMBINATION METER ASSEMBLY  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Instrument Cluster / Carrier/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Removal
CONNECT CONNECTOR (a) Reconnect the E70 No. 10 junction connector connector. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR OPEN IN CAN BUS MAIN WIRE (NO. 9 JUNCTION CONNECTOR - NO. 10 JUNCTION CONNECTOR) (a) Disconnect the E73 No. 9 junction connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Part 2 Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE CAN MAIN WIRE OR CONNECTOR (NO. 9 JUNCTION CONNECTOR - NO. 10 JUNCTION CONNECTOR) OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR OPEN IN CAN BUS MAIN WIRE (NO. 2 CAN JUNCTION CONNECTOR - NO. 9 JUNCTION CONNECTOR) (a) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE CAN MAIN WIRE OR CONNECTOR (NO. 2 CAN JUNCTION CONNECTOR - NO. 9 JUNCTION CONNECTOR) OK --  REPAIR OR REPLACE NO. 9 JUNCTION CONNECTOR

CHECK FOR SHORT IN CAN BUS WIRES (NO. 10 JUNCTION CONNECTOR SIDE) (a) Disconnect the E70 No. 10 junction connector connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  CONNECT CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR SHORT IN CAN BUS WIRES (NO. 10 JUNCTION CONNECTOR - COMBINATION METER) (a) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  CONNECT CONNECTOR OK --  REPAIR OR REPLACE NO. 10 JUNCTION CONNECTOR
CONNECT CONNECTOR (a) Reconnect the E70 No. 10 junction connector connector. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR SHORT IN CAN BUS WIRES (NO. 10 JUNCTION CONNECTOR - NO. 9 JUNCTION CONNECTOR) (a) Disconnect the E73 No. 9 junction connector connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE CAN MAIN WIRE OR CONNECTOR (NO. 10 JUNCTION CONNECTOR - NO. 9 JUNCTION CONNECTOR) OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR SHORT IN CAN BUS WIRES (NO. 9 JUNCTION CONNECTOR SIDE) (a) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  CONNECT CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR SHORT IN CAN BUS WIRES (NO. 9 JUNCTION CONNECTOR - WINDSHIELD WIPER ECU) (a) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  CONNECT CONNECTOR OK --  REPAIR OR REPLACE NO. 9 JUNCTION CONNECTOR
CONNECT CONNECTOR (a) Reconnect the E73 No. 9 junction connector connector. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR SHORT IN CAN BUS WIRES (NO. 2 CAN JUNCTION CONNECTOR - NO. 9 JUNCTION CONNECTOR) (a) Disconnect the L30 No. 2 CAN junction connector connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE CAN MAIN WIRE OR CONNECTOR (NO. 2 CAN JUNCTION CONNECTOR - NO. 9 JUNCTION CONNECTOR) OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR SHORT IN CAN BUS WIRES (NO. 2 CAN JUNCTION CONNECTOR SIDE) (a) Disconnect the L29 No. 2 CAN junction connector connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  CONNECT CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.
CONNECT CONNECTOR (a) Reconnect the L29 and L30 No. 2 CAN junction connector connectors. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR SHORT IN CAN BUS WIRES (NO. 2 CAN JUNCTION CONNECTOR - TIRE PRESSURE WARNING ECU) (a) Disconnect the L31 No. 2 CAN junction connector connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  CONNECT CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR SHORT IN CAN BUS WIRES (NO. 2 CAN JUNCTION CONNECTOR - POWER BACK DOOR UNIT) HINT For vehicles without the power back door system, go to "Check for Short in CAN Bus Wires (No. 2 CAN Junction Connector - No. 2 Main Body ECU)". (a) Disconnect the L45 No. 2 junction connector connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  CONNECT CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR SHORT IN CAN BUS WIRES (NO. 2 CAN JUNCTION CONNECTOR - NO. 2 MAIN BODY ECU) (a) Disconnect the L46 No. 2 junction connector connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  CONNECT CONNECTOR OK --  REPLACE NO. 2 CAN JUNCTION CONNECTOR
CONNECT CONNECTOR (a) Reconnect the L29 and L30 No. 2 CAN junction connector connectors. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR SHORT IN CAN BUS WIRES (NO. 2 CAN JUNCTION CONNECTOR - NO. 8 JUNCTION CONNECTOR) (a) Disconnect the E76 No. 8 junction connector connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE CAN MAIN WIRE OR CONNECTOR (NO. 8 JUNCTION CONNECTOR - NO. 2 CAN JUNCTION CONNECTOR) OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR SHORT IN CAN BUS WIRES (NO. 8 JUNCTION CONNECTOR - CERTIFICATION ECU) (a) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  CONNECT CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR SHORT IN CAN BUS WIRES (NO. 8 JUNCTION CONNECTOR - POSITION CONTROL ECU AND SWITCH) (a) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Part 3 Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  CONNECT CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR SHORT IN CAN BUS WIRES (NO. 8 JUNCTION CONNECTOR - MULTIPLEX TILT AND TELESCOPIC ECU) (a) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  CONNECT CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR SHORT IN CAN BUS WIRES (NO. 8 JUNCTION CONNECTOR - OUTER MIRROR CONTROL ECU) (a) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  CONNECT CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR SHORT IN CAN BUS WIRES (NO. 8 JUNCTION CONNECTOR - MAIN BODY ECU) (a) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  CONNECT CONNECTOR OK --  REPAIR OR REPLACE NO. 8 JUNCTION CONNECTOR
CONNECT CONNECTOR (a) Reconnect the E70 No. 10 junction connector connector. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR SHORT IN CAN BUS WIRES (COMBINATION METER) (a) Disconnect the E77 combination meter connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE CAN MAIN WIRE CONNECTED TO COMBINATION METER (MPX+, MPX-) OK --  REPLACE COMBINATION METER ASSEMBLY  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Instrument Cluster / Carrier/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Removal
CONNECT CONNECTOR (a) Reconnect the E73 No. 9 junction connector connector. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR SHORT IN CAN BUS WIRES (WINDSHIELD WIPER ECU) (a) Disconnect the E90 windshield wiper ECU connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE CAN BRANCH WIRE CONNECTED TO WINDSHIELD WIPER ECU (CANH, CANL) OK --  REPLACE WINDSHIELD WIPER ECU  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Wiper and Washer Systems/Wiper Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal
CONNECT CONNECTOR (a) Reconnect the L31 No. 2 CAN junction connector connector. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR SHORT IN CAN BUS WIRES (TIRE PRESSURE WARNING ECU) (a) Disconnect the L10 tire pressure warning ECU connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE CAN BRANCH WIRE CONNECTED TO TIRE PRESSURE WARNING ECU (CANP, CANN) OK --  REPLACE TIRE PRESSURE WARNING ECU  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Tire Pressure Module/Service and Repair/Removal
CONNECT CONNECTOR (a) Reconnect the L31 and L45 No. 2 CAN junction connector connectors. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR SHORT IN CAN BUS WIRES (POWER BACK DOOR UNIT) (a) Disconnect the L36 power back door unit (power back door ECU) connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE CAN BRANCH WIRE CONNECTED TO POWER BACK DOOR UNIT (POWER BACK DOOR ECU) (MPX1, MPX2) OK --  REPLACE POWER BACK DOOR UNIT ASSEMBLY (POWER BACK DOOR ECU)  See: Body and Frame/Doors, Hood and Trunk/Trunk / Liftgate/Trunk / Liftgate Motor/Service and Repair/Removal
CONNECT CONNECTOR (a) Reconnect the L31, L45 and L46 No. 2 CAN junction connector connectors. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR SHORT IN CAN BUS WIRES (NO. 2 MAIN BODY ECU) (a) Disconnect the L33 No. 2 main body ECU connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE CAN BRANCH WIRE CONNECTED TO NO. 2 MAIN BODY ECU (MPX+, MPX-) OK --  REPLACE NO. 2 MAIN BODY ECU  See: Body and Frame/Interior Moulding / Trim/Headliner/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Removal
CONNECT CONNECTOR (a) Reconnect the E76 No. 8 junction connector connector. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR SHORT IN CAN BUS WIRES (CERTIFICATION ECU (SMART KEY ECU ASSEMBLY)) (a) Disconnect the E29 certification ECU (smart key ECU) connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE CAN BRANCH WIRE CONNECTED TO CERTIFICATION ECU (SMART KEY ECU ASSEMBLY) (CANH, CANL) OK --  REPLACE CERTIFICATION ECU (SMART KEY ECU ASSEMBLY)  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Alarm Module/Service and Repair/Removal
CONNECT CONNECTOR (a) Reconnect the E76 No. 8 junction connector connector. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR SHORT IN CAN BUS WIRES (POSITION CONTROL ECU AND SWITCH) (a) Disconnect the c15 position control ECU and switch connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE CAN BRANCH WIRE CONNECTED TO POSITION CONTROL ECU AND SWITCH (MPX1, MPX2) OK --  REPLACE POSITION CONTROL ECU AND SWITCH ASSEMBLY  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Power Seat Switch/Service and Repair/Power Seat Switch (for Front Passenger Side)/Removal
CONNECT CONNECTOR (a) Reconnect the E76 No. 8 junction connector connector. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR SHORT IN CAN BUS WIRES (MULTIPLEX TILT AND TELESCOPIC ECU) (a) Disconnect the E27 multiplex tilt and telescopic ECU connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE CAN BRANCH WIRE CONNECTED TO MULTIPLEX TILT AND TELESCOPIC ECU (CANP, CANN) OK --  REPLACE MULTIPLEX TILT AND TELESCOPIC ECU  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Steering and Suspension/Relays and Modules - Steering/Steering Column Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal
CONNECT CONNECTOR (a) Reconnect the E76 No. 8 junction connector connector. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR SHORT IN CAN BUS WIRES (OUTER MIRROR CONTROL ECU) (a) Disconnect the I18 outer mirror control ECU connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE CAN BRANCH WIRE CONNECTED TO OUTER MIRROR CONTROL ECU (CANH, CANL) OK --  REPLACE OUTER MIRROR CONTROL ECU ASSEMBLY  See: Body and Frame/Mirrors/Power Mirror Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal
CONNECT CONNECTOR (a) Reconnect the E76 No. 8 junction connector connector. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR SHORT IN CAN BUS WIRES (MAIN BODY ECU) (a) Disconnect the E3 main body ECU (cowl side junction block LH) connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE CAN MAIN WIRE CONNECTED TO MAIN BODY ECU (COWL SIDE JUNCTION BLOCK LH) (CANP, CANN) OK --  REPLACE MAIN BODY ECU (COWL SIDE JUNCTION BLOCK LH)

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (MAIN BODY ECU - BATTERY AND BODY GROUND) (a) Connect the cable to the negative (-) battery terminal. NOTICE: When disconnecting the cable, some systems need to be initialized after the cable is reconnected    See: Testing and Inspection/Repair Instruction - Initialization. Text in Illustration (b) Disconnect the 2A, 2D and E3 main body ECU (cowl side junction block LH) connectors. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: (d) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Voltage: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK --  REPLACE MAIN BODY ECU (COWL SIDE JUNCTION BLOCK LH) Part 1 NETWORKING: CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM: U1002 (CAN No. 2 BUS): Lost Communication with Gateway Module (Network Gateway ECU) U1002 (CAN No. 2 BUS) - Lost Communication with Gateway Module (Network Gateway ECU) PROCEDURE
CONNECT CONNECTOR (a) Reconnect the E71 No. 4 junction connector connector. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR SHORT IN CAN BUS WIRES (NETWORK GATEWAY ECU) (a) Disconnect the E31 network gateway ECU connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE CAN BRANCH WIRE CONNECTED TO NETWORK GATEWAY ECU (CA2H, CA2L) OK --  REPLACE NETWORK GATEWAY ECU  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Central Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (NETWORK GATEWAY ECU - BATTERY AND BODY GROUND) (a) Connect the cable to the negative (-) battery terminal. NOTICE: When disconnecting the cable, some systems need to be initialized after the cable is reconnected    See: Testing and Inspection/Repair Instruction - Initialization. (b) Disconnect the E31 network gateway ECU connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: (d) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Voltage: Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK --  REPLACE NETWORK GATEWAY ECU  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Central Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal

dtcs.description: Lost Communication with Seat Belt Control ECU HINT For vehicles with the dynamic radar cruise control system only. NOTICE: Inspect the fuses for circuits related to this system before performing the following inspection procedure. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: DISCONNECT CABLE FROM NEGATIVE BATTERY TERMINAL (a) Disconnect the cable from the negative (-) battery terminal before measuring the resistances of the main wire and the branch wire.

CHECK FOR OPEN IN CAN BUS WIRE (SEAT BELT CONTROL ECU BRANCH WIRE) (a) Disconnect the E42 seat belt control ECU connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE SEAT BELT CONTROL ECU BRANCH WIRE OR CONNECTOR (CANH, CANL) OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (SEAT BELT CONTROL ECU - BATTERY AND BODY GROUND) (a) Connect the cable to the negative (-) battery terminal. NOTICE: When disconnecting the cable, some systems need to be initialized after the cable is reconnected    See: Testing and Inspection/Repair Instruction - Initialization. (b) Disconnect the E41 and E42 seat belt control ECU connectors. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: (d) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Voltage: Text in Illustration NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK --  REPLACE SEAT BELT CONTROL ECU  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Restraints and Safety Systems/Seat Belt Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal Dynamic Radar Cruise Control System
